steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that there can be "significant within-species variations in the number of individuals… over time." The example must illustrate one species whose count changes dramatically across the study period — not a comparison between two species, and not a description that undersells the variation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'striated rockskipper population was at its highest in January and April… lowest in July': describes within-species variation but gives no numbers, so we can't tell if the swing is "significant." ✗
  • Choice B — 'Indo-Pacific sergeant population ranged from a low of 2 individuals in January to a high of 28 individuals in October': one species, exact numbers, a 14-fold swing — a striking range that directly exemplifies significant within-species variation. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Cocos frillgoby population reached its highest level in April… when 10 individuals were observed': only gives the peak, not the range. Doesn't demonstrate the extent of variation. ✗
  • Choice D — 'striated rockskipper population never exceeded 5… whereas the Indo-Pacific sergeant population reached 28': compares two different species — that's between-species variation, not within-species variation. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
